column1: # UnifiedFloppyTool canonical error-code table (SSOT for Fact 3).
# This file is the ONLY source of truth for UFT error codes.
# docs/ERRORS.md) are generated from this file via
#   The enum member identifier is `UFT_ERR_<NAME>` (matches existing ABI
#   in include/uft/uft_error.h — 848 uses in 76 files). The long-form
#   alias `UFT_ERROR_<NAME>` is emitted as a preprocessor #define in
#   that use the mixed-case spelling. Do NOT flip this; doing so breaks
#   code         - signed decimal integer, negative for errors. 0 reserved for UFT_SUCCESS.
#   name         - short identifier appended to UFT_ERR_ (e.g. "IO" -> UFT_ERR_IO).
#   category     - one of: ARG, IO, FORMAT, RESOURCE, FEATURE, HARDWARE, INTERNAL, MISC.
#   description  - one-line human-readable (used in uft_strerror and docs).
#   The numeric code of every SAFE token (14 listed below) MUST match the
#   value in the pre-SSOT uft_error.h. Changing a code is an ABI break
# Lines starting with '#' are comments. Blank lines are allowed.
# Rows marked "# PROPOSED" above them are awaiting human decision
# (see consult block in single-source-enforcer dispatch output).
# === SAFE: direct carry-over from canonical uft_error.h ===================

column4: Invalid argument (NULL pointer, out of range, etc.)
Operation not permitted in current state

column1: # === PROPOSED: new canonical entries for the 31 UNSAFE legacy tokens =====
# Each row below is PROPOSED by single-source-enforcer. Human review of the
# consolidated CONSULT block is required before promotion. If accepted as-is,
# delete the "# PROPOSED" comment above the row to confirm.
# The generator (gen_errors_h.py) will ACCEPT PROPOSED rows (they appear in
# the emitted enum) but will EMIT a compile-time #pragma message listing
# each proposed row so drift stays visible in every build log.
# PROPOSED: resolves NOMEM vs MEMORY vs OUT_OF_MEMORY triplet.
# Canonical stays UFT_ERR_MEMORY (code -30); the other two become compat
# defines in uft_error_compat_gen.h, not distinct enum members.
# No new row needed — handled by alias generation.
# --- FORMAT extension (semantic-correctness fix for current compat bug) --
# PROPOSED: new canonical for "format structurally invalid" which the old
# compat header mis-mapped to CORRUPTED (semantic violation documented in
# dispatch). Adds a distinct code so callers can differentiate
# "data is garbled" (CORRUPTED) from "file claims to be IMD but isn't"

column4: Format could not be identified from content

column1: # --- FORMAT subcodes moved from compat to canonical ----------------------
# PROPOSED: these were previously aliased to CORRUPTED or FILE_NOT_FOUND
# by uft_unified_types.h — losing semantic info. Giving them distinct

column4: Weak bits detected in data field
Expected sector not found on track
Copy refused due to protection policy

column1: # PROPOSED: distinct code for "verify operation failed" (was aliased to CRC).
# Callers that verify a written image care whether they hit CRC mismatch
# (media problem) vs. byte-level compare mismatch (write problem).

column4: Operation cancelled by user or policy
Object/context in invalid state for operation
